result:
Universal, durable empathy.

Not just “people being nicer,” but a real increase in the ability to feel the reality of other people’s inner lives — across class, nation, ideology, and time. If more people could genuinely experience “that person is as real as I am,” a lot of the worst things humans do would become harder to justify and easier to stop.

It wouldn’t solve everything. We’d still have scarcity, conflict, disasters, and bad incentives. But it would change how we build systems, how we use power, and what we’re willing to tolerate.

If you want, I can also answer the darker version: what one change would be most dangerous.
prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
